title: rubus scaber var. macrostachys
intro: Rubus scaber var. macrostachys, also known as the Japanese bramble or wild raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus scaber plant, which is a type of thorny shrub native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus scaber var. macrostachys include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce cholesterol levels, and alleviate symptoms of certain gastrointestinal disorders, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers. Rubus scaber var. macrostachys can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusement to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
